**Instructions**: Log into your Anthropi: **Instructions**: Log into your Anthropic account, go to API Keys, click Create Key
1. Use the <b>arrow keys</b> to find <b>:
1. Use the arrow keys to find "Snapshots" in the boot menu
2. Press Enter to expand the list of available snapshots
3. Choose the snapshot taken before the update
4. Press Enter to boot into that snapshot
5. Test your system — if issues persist, try an earlier snapshot
6. When satisfied, return here and run Restore Snapshot to make it permanent
7. Reboot and boot as normal
10-bit colour is not supported by this d: 10-bit colour is not supported by this display or driver.
A theme is a snapshot of your current lo: A theme is a snapshot of your current look — wallpaper, colors, UI changes, and window decorations. Tap "Save current as theme" to capture what's on screen, then switch between saved themes any time with one tap. Use "Update" on the active theme to overwrite it with your latest tweaks.
API Keys can be created here: www.unspla: API Keys can be created here: www.unsplash.com/developers
Account created! They can now sign in.
Adjusts the colour saturation of SDR con:
Adjusts the colour saturation of SDR content. 1.0 is accurate sRGB; increase slightly if SDR apps look desaturated next to HDR, or decrease if they look over-vivid.

The colour swatches update in real time.
Aligns the date and quote to left, cente: Aligns the date and quote to left, center or right depending on its position on the screen.
All windows float freely on the desktop: All windows float freely on the desktop
Also delete their files and folders: Also delete their files and folders
An active ICC profile disables all other: An active ICC profile disables all other color management
An internet connection is required for f: An internet connection is required for full installation. Please connect to Wi-Fi below before starting the installer.
Are you sure you want to remove the acco: Are you sure you want to remove the account "
Audio input | Right-click for volume mix: Audio input | Right-click for volume mixer & device selector

Automatically say yes to prompts during: Automatically say yes to prompts during update
Background blur behind transparent windo: Background blur behind transparent windows and layers
Before anything installs, a snapshot of: Before anything installs, a snapshot of your entire system is saved automatically — this is your safety net. If something ever goes wrong after updating, the Recovery page will walk you through rolling back to exactly how your system was before the update.
Before you click Update, take a moment t: Before you click Update, take a moment to test anything important — printers, audio, external drives, browsers, or any apps you rely on daily. After the update completes, test those same things again. Most updates go smoothly, but it's good to know right away if something needs attention.
Black floor · %1 nits — raise until li: Black floor  ·  %1 nits — raise until lightest shadow bar becomes just visible
Can only be turned on using the 'Dots' o: Can only be turned on using the 'Dots' or 'Full' dial style for aesthetic reasons
Can't be turned on when using 'Numbers': Can't be turned on when using 'Numbers' dial style for aesthetic reasons

Choose a starting preset, then fine-tune: Choose a starting preset, then fine-tune each value:

Clicking Apply will save these values to monitors.conf
and reload Hyprland immediately.

This is done with an image processing algorithm run locally and no AI is used.

Could not update the login image.

Lifts the black floor for SDR content on:
Lifts the black floor for SDR content only, recovering shadow detail that can be crushed during the SDR→HDR conversion. Values of 0.003–0.01 suit most displays.

Increase until the checkerboard texture in the dark area becomes just visible.
Locale code, e.g. fr_FR, de_DE, zh_CN...

but has less impressive morphing

Makes the clock always rotate. This is e:
Makes the clock always rotate. This is extremely expensive
(expect 50% usage on Intel UHD Graphics) and thus impractical.
Monitors reduce brightness when large br:
Monitors reduce brightness when large bright areas are displayed for extended periods (ABL / APL limiting). Set this to 60–70 % of your peak brightness.

The large patch simulates a bright scene. Typical values: 300–600 nits.

Scales the overall brightness of SDR con:
Scales the overall brightness of SDR content in HDR mode. 1.0 is the reference level; increase if SDR applications look dim compared to native HDR content.

The grey ramp updates in real time to show how all SDR tones shift.
See setup instructions on the wiki: See setup instructions on the wiki
Set this to your display's rated peak br:
Set this to your display's rated peak brightness in nits.

The small centre patch represents a brief HDR highlight — on a real HDR display it would be blindingly bright. Look up your monitor's Peak Luminance specification (often listed as HDR400 / HDR600 / HDR1000 / HDR1400).

The reference bar at the bottom shows common display tiers.

The absolute minimum brightness your pan:
The absolute minimum brightness your panel can produce. OLED and QD-OLED panels can reach true 0; LCD panels typically fall between 0.02 and 0.1 nits.

Increase until the second-lightest shadow bar is just barely visible. Leave at 0 for OLED/QD-OLED.
The brightness of 100 % white in SDR app:
The brightness of 100 % white in SDR applications (the 'paper white' reference). The broadcast standard is 203 nits; a comfortable desktop value is 250–350 nits.

Raise if SDR windows look too dim next to HDR content. Lower if SDR feels too harsh.

This wizard guides you through calibrati:
This wizard guides you through calibrating the HDR luminance settings for

    %1

For best results:

  •  Dim the room — avoid bright light falling on the screen
  •  Let the monitor warm up for 20–30 minutes
  •  Disable auto-brightness and ambient light sensors
